decisions.relationship_evidence: This is a single clinical case report describing mNGS diagnosis and possible underrecognition; it does not evaluate a case definition, reporting rule, or diagnostic-validity method.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The record is neither a methodological source nor a study of case-definition or reporting validity.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Clinical and paraclinical pediatric tuberculosis data were analyzed with latent class analysis to estimate diagnostic accuracy and construct a treatment rule.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application of latent class analysis to pediatric tuberculosis diagnosis in Rwanda.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study used latent class analysis without a gold standard to estimate pneumococcal pneumonia attribution and test accuracy under antimicrobial exposure.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to pneumococcal pneumonia diagnosis in Kenyan adults.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Published melioidosis test data were reanalyzed with Bayesian latent class models to estimate culture sensitivity and misclassification effects under an imperfect gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete diagnostic application to melioidosis and alternative-test evaluation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class models were used to estimate the sensitivity and specificity of three bovine tuberculosis tests where true infection status was unknown.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete field application to bovine tuberculosis diagnostics in Ireland.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class models estimated somatic-cell-count and culture performance for Staphylococcus aureus infection in the absence of a gold-standard test.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to subclinical S. aureus intramammary infection in goats.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis, including a clustered model, estimated trachoma-test sensitivity and specificity without a gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to laboratory and clinical trachoma tests in Ethiopia.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study explicitly extended the conventional two-class latent class model to three infection-stage classes within a Bayesian framework.
decisions.lineage_evidence: It contains an explicit methodological extension of the diagnostic latent-class model, demonstrated on infectious salmon anaemia testing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models were used to estimate sensitivity and specificity distributions for feline immunodeficiency virus PCR and serological tests.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete validation application involving FIV diagnostics in domestic cats.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Leptospirosis test data were reevaluated with Bayesian latent class models to account for an imperfect culture-plus-MAT reference standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete reanalysis of five leptospirosis diagnostic tests.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models estimated herd-level sensitivity and specificity of PCR and culture for Streptococcus agalactiae.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to national dairy-herd surveillance testing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models without a reference test estimated malaria prevalence and the accuracy of RDT, microscopy, and PCR.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to malaria diagnostic data from São Tomé and Príncipe.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Modified latent class model analyses estimated age-specific error rates of HPV, cytology, and histology tests without gold-standard diagnoses.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HPV-related cervical-screening application of a modified latent-class model.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis and the Hui-Walter model estimated PCR and culture properties for S. aureus infection without a perfect reference test.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to bovine intramammary-infection testing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis estimated real-time PCR and culture sensitivity and specificity for S. agalactiae while avoiding a perfect-reference assumption.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete dairy-cattle diagnostic application across PCR cutoffs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Dengue reference and rapid-test data were reevaluated using Bayesian latent class models because the reference assay was imperfect.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to dengue diagnostic-test accuracy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Bayesian latent class approach estimated commercial ELISA accuracy for Toxoplasma gondii infection without an available gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to naturally infected pig populations.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis estimated diagnostic sensitivity and specificity for Squash vein yellowing virus when no gold-standard test existed.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete plant-virus diagnostic application across tissues, hosts, and habitats.

decisions.relationship_evidence: This systematic review directly evaluated diagnostic latent-class methodology and reporting, identifying unchecked conditional-independence assumptions and inadequate model-fit evidence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Methodological review providing explicit cautions and reporting guidance for diagnostic latent-class studies.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent-class methods estimated somatic-cell-count and culture accuracy and conditional dependence for intramammary infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to major mastitis-pathogen detection in dairy cows.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis evaluated the accuracy of serological, parasitological, and qPCR tests for canine visceral leishmaniasis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Leishmania infantum diagnosis in dogs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models without a gold standard estimated malaria-test sensitivity, specificity, and submicroscopic-infection prevalence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to pooled PCR testing in pregnant Malawian women.
supported_label_variants: latent class models without a gold standard

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analyses estimated bulk-tank-milk ELISA and PCR performance because neither test was a gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete herd-level application to Mycoplasma bovis diagnosis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Bayesian latent class model without a gold-standard test estimated culture and ELISA performance across Johne's disease shedding stages.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Mycobacterium avium subsp. paratuberculosis testing in cattle.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A latent class model estimated PCR and serology performance for several respiratory infections where no gold standard existed.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to adenovirus, pertussis, and influenza diagnostics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis was used to assess canine leishmaniasis tests independently of an inadequate reference standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete evaluation of microscopy, IFAT, LAMP, and PCR in dogs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models accounting for herd disease dynamics compared two PCR tests for Coxiella burnetii.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to dairy-farm C. burnetii detection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A latent class regression approach simultaneously estimated six brucellosis-test accuracies, true prevalence, clustering, and risk factors without a gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Brucella abortus field-trial data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated the accuracy of five childhood pulmonary tuberculosis tests in the absence of an accurate reference test.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to hospitalized South African children with suspected tuberculosis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis simultaneously estimated PCR and culture sensitivity and specificity for four intramammary pathogens.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to dairy-cow intramammary-infection diagnosis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper explicitly developed STARD-BLCM, a modified reporting guideline for diagnostic-accuracy studies using Bayesian latent class models without a reference standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological reporting guideline with examples and elaborations for Bayesian latent-class studies.
named_source_mentions: Standards for the Reporting of Diagnostic Accuracy (STARD) statement

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis was used alongside microscopy and a composite reference standard to assess schistosomiasis screening-test accuracy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to schistosomiasis screening in African migrants.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis evaluated liver-biopsy and noninvasive fibrosis methods in chronic hepatitis C without a gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete HCV-related diagnostic-staging application.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis supplied an internal standard to compare microscopy, RDT, and PCR for malaria diagnosis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete malaria diagnostic application in a hospital population.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Bayesian latent class meta-analysis estimated TST and interferon-gamma-release-assay performance for latent tuberculosis infection without a gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete meta-analytic application to LTBI diagnostics across population groups.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent-class models estimated four fox diagnostic-test accuracies, prevalence, covariate effects, and empirical cutoffs without a perfect gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Echinococcus multilocularis diagnosis in Swiss foxes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models estimated pneumococcal-pneumonia prevalence and compared three diagnostic assays.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to vaccine-serotype pneumococcal pneumonia diagnosis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class modeling compared tissue and sonicate-fluid culture accuracy without assuming a gold standard for prosthetic-joint infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to prosthetic-joint-infection diagnosis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models estimated multiplex-immunoassay and ELISA characteristics because none of the bovine respiratory tests was considered perfect.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete herd-level application to bovine respiratory syncytial virus and coronavirus testing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models evaluated qPCR and bacterial culture for S. agalactiae because neither was a perfect reference test.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to cow composite-milk diagnosis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis assessed the relative accuracy of stool examination, serology, and urine transrenal-DNA PCR for strongyloidiasis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete field application to Strongyloides stercoralis diagnosis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study explicitly compared standard and extended latent class models, examined fit and assumptions, and identified problems from data sparseness and software limitations.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct methodological assessment of diagnostic latent-class model specification and performance, illustrated with Campylobacter tests.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated sensitivity, specificity, and prevalence for three latent-tuberculosis tests lacking a gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to a large US cohort at risk for latent tuberculosis infection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Bayesian latent class model estimated fecal-culture and serum-ELISA accuracy for paratuberculosis, allowing conditional test dependence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Mycobacterium avium subsp. paratuberculosis in dairy cows.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analyses compared ELISA and RT-rPCR accuracy where no perfect reference standard existed.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to subclinical Renibacterium salmoninarum infection in salmon broodstock.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulated data with known prevalence were used to evaluate Bayesian latent class models, revealing prior sensitivity, overestimation, and poor performance under low prevalence and sensitivity.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct simulation-based performance assessment of the exact method.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated PCR and culture performance across composite and quarter milk samples without a reference standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Staphylococcus aureus intramammary infection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A latent class model estimated TST and interferon-gamma-release-assay performance for latent tuberculosis infection in an endemic setting.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to healthcare workers in a tuberculosis- and HIV-endemic setting.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis constructed a reference standard and evaluated three Helicobacter pylori tests when no reference standard was available.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to H. pylori diagnostic testing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated direct-PCR and qPCR accuracy for a newly discovered pathogen in the absence of a gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to porcine circovirus 3 diagnostics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Bayesian latent class model compared culture, immunofluorescence, and PCR sensitivity and specificity for adenoviral conjunctivitis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to human adenovirus diagnostic testing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis estimated the performance of two ELISAs and Western blot in a blinded inter-laboratory Mycoplasma bovis trial.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to bovine M. bovis serodiagnosis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated diagnostic sensitivity and specificity for five salmonid alphavirus tests where no gold standard existed.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete field application to salmonid alphavirus and pancreas-disease testing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Bayesian latent class model estimated PMS-PCR and ELISA sensitivity and specificity for subclinical paratuberculosis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Mycobacterium avium subsp. paratuberculosis diagnosis in cattle.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Simulations directly examined when fixed test specificity, sample size, and prevalence cause Bayesian latent class analysis to improve or bias estimates.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit performance and bias evaluation of the method, supplemented by a wildlife-disease case study.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated latent-tuberculosis prevalence and three test characteristics in US-born people with HIV.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to latent-tuberculosis testing strategy.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis used four assay results and geographic origin to infer Trypanosoma cruzi serostatus and test accuracy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to chronic Chagas disease diagnosis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Bayesian latent class model estimated three caprine-mastitis screening tests and true prevalence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to subclinical caprine mastitis in Bangladesh.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis combined laboratory and clinical variables without a gold standard to derive C. difficile diagnostic-probability zones.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Clostridioides difficile diagnosis and PCR threshold interpretation.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models estimated four ELISA and fecal-PCR characteristics while accounting for conditional dependence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to paratuberculosis diagnosis in New Zealand dairy cattle.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis estimated the diagnostic performance of three serological tests for Leishmania infantum infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to canine leishmaniasis testing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis reevaluated dipstick-test accuracy for urinary tract infection without treating the reference procedure as perfect.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to urinary-tract-infection diagnostic tests.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Real-data analyses and extensive simulations examined conditional-independence violations, zero inflation, partial gold standards, model fit, bias, and accuracy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Direct methodological assessment of latent-class diagnostic-study design and analysis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated histopathology, PCR, and qPCR accuracy for detecting Mikrocytos mackini without a perfect reference test.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to an infectious oyster disease.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class models evaluated the diagnostic performance of three commercial ELISAs for Coxiella burnetii infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to serological diagnosis in domestic ruminants.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated PCR and IFAT sensitivity and specificity without a reference test for Theileria annulata.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to asymptomatic cattle and buffaloes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models estimated two bulk-tank-milk ELISA tests against claw-trimming records for bovine digital dermatitis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete herd-level application to an infectious bovine claw disease.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis compared whole-genome-sequencing and culture-based antimicrobial-susceptibility testing without designating a gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to antimicrobial-resistance monitoring in livestock E. coli.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis combined three antibody-test results to estimate SARS-CoV-2 seroprevalence, underreporting, and infection-fatality ratios.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete population-level application to SARS-CoV-2 serology.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Bayesian latent class model estimated three bovine-tuberculosis tests and true within-herd prevalence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Mycobacterium bovis diagnosis in Egyptian dairy cattle.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated ovine echinococcosis prevalence and evaluated a novel ELISA while accounting for diagnostic uncertainty.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete surveillance application to Echinococcus granulosus infection in sheep.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper analyzes synthetic and published data with continuous-outcome Bayesian finite mixture models and explicitly equates these with Bayesian latent class models.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Provides a practical guide and explicit recommendation for continuous rather than dichotomized diagnostic outcomes.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models combined dichotomous PCR with continuous serology-reader data to improve disease classification without a gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to canine visceral leishmaniosis diagnostics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A latent class model estimated three serological-test characteristics and selected an optimal ELISA cutoff without a gold-standard test.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Toxoplasma gondii diagnosis in pigs.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models without a reference standard estimated sensitivity and specificity of common salmon gill-disease tests.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete field application to amoebic and complex gill disease.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated ELISA and trans-PCR diagnostic sensitivity and specificity for Coxiella burnetii.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to cattle and buffaloes in India.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models jointly estimated prevalence and accuracy of three canine Leishmania serological tests.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete two-country application to canine Leishmania exposure.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class modelling treated all three leptospirosis tests as imperfect and compared its results with an MAT-gold-standard analysis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to human leptospirosis point-of-care tests.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis with conditional test dependence estimated three maedi-visna ELISA accuracies without a perfect reference test.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to maedi-visna virus surveillance in sheep.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models estimated three serological-test accuracies and true Brucella prevalence in camels.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to camel brucellosis diagnosis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated culture, qPCR, and somatic-cell-count performance for S. aureus intramammary infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to mastitis diagnosis in goats.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models estimated the accuracies of two point-of-care culture systems and laboratory culture for Streptococcus uberis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to mastitis diagnostics in dairy cows.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis estimated prevalence and accuracy for 11 Schistosoma mansoni tests and informed combined diagnostic strategies.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application in a low-endemicity Brazilian population.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Bayesian latent class model compared IMS-qPCR with conventional qPCR to infer infectious status and diagnostic accuracy.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to pathogenic Leptospira detection in dairy-cattle urine.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The paper proposed and evaluated a flexible Bayesian latent-class model that explicitly accommodates conditional dependence among both true-positive and true-negative groups.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Explicit methodological development, validated on one tuberculosis dataset and applied to another.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis evaluated paired ELISA and qPCR results for Mycoplasma bovis in the absence of a gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete diagnostic-test evaluation supporting New Zealand's eradication program.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A latent class meta-analysis estimated cholera-test sensitivity and specificity before adjustment of suspected-case positivity for test performance.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete meta-analytic application to Vibrio cholerae surveillance data.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models jointly estimated seven SARS-CoV-2 serological-test accuracies and compared results with gold-standard and frequentist analyses.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to SARS-CoV-2 antibody diagnostics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A latent class model on three imperfect mycobacterial tests supplied unknown disease status for development of a tuberculous-meningitis prediction model.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to tuberculous-meningitis diagnosis; the novel component is the clinical prediction model, not latent-class methodology itself.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated prevalence and age-stratified accuracy of Kato-Katz and CCA tests where an effective gold standard was absent.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Schistosoma japonicum diagnosis in the Philippines.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class models estimated herd-level accuracy of abattoir registrations and bulk-tank-milk ELISA for liver-fluke infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete surveillance application to Fasciola hepatica in Norwegian cattle herds.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis estimated PCR and two ELISA characteristics for herd-level Mycoplasma bovis infection.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to routine bulk-tank-milk surveillance in Ireland.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis without a gold-standard test compared three molecular assays and estimated Coxiella-positive tick prevalence.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Coxiella burnetii detection in Kenyan ticks.

decisions.relationship_evidence: The study uses latent class trajectory analysis to group longitudinal HBsAg patterns, not diagnostic latent-class modeling of imperfect tests without a gold standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: The trajectory model is a neighboring but distinct latent-class concept.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Bayesian latent class analysis was used alongside a clinical gold-standard analysis to estimate molecular-assay sensitivity and specificity where no laboratory gold standard existed.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to amoebic liver abscess diagnostics.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Bayesian latent class model adapted from the Hui-Walter model estimated three liver-fluke test characteristics in two dairy herds.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to Fasciola hepatica diagnosis in New Zealand cattle.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A three-test, two-population Bayesian latent class analysis estimated dependent serological-test performance for Brucella suis.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to canine brucellosis diagnosis.

decisions.relationship_evidence: Latent class analysis inferred HPV infection status and estimated accuracy indices for two molecular-detection kits.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to high-risk HPV diagnostic testing.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A Bayesian latent class model estimated the diagnostic accuracy of three tests for naturally exposed cattle.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to bovine Besnoitia besnoiti infection.

decisions.relationship_evidence: A four-test Bayesian latent class model with conditional PCR dependence estimated diagnostic accuracy without assuming a reference standard.
decisions.lineage_evidence: Concrete application to bovine ephemeral fever virus infection in South African cattle.
